AOM_v0 champion: median 0.960 rel-RMSEP, ~42/57 wins vs PLS-std.
HPO does not improve median (cf. AOM_v0/Summary §6).

Anchor: every preset includes a tuned PLS as denominator for
`score_ratio_vs_source_run_pls`. n_components selected by 5-fold CV.
Slated for unification with AOM-PLS per AOM/ROADMAP M1.

Equal-weight average of 4 bases. inner_cv_nested=false because the
ensemble does not fit a meta on Y; exporter must keep it out of
`strong_practical` per the gating rule.
47/61 vs PLS-std (multiview/docs/SUMMARY §6).
Paper reference; not runnable in this registry. Harness must refuse to dispatch.

Kernel ridge (n×n) — OOM risk when n_train > 3000. For larger
cohorts use AOMRidge-Local-compact-knn50.
k-NN local kernel; safe for n_train > 3000.

`module` path uses a hyphen; harness must quote-and-escape it.
Locked but ranked below AOM-Ridge for `strong_practical`.
Hybrid CNN+linear stack. Pending B's residual gate.

Pending A2 nested audit (D-A-001) and ≥3-seed multi-seed rerun
before promotion to `strong_practical`. 128 source rows, 53
datasets, single seed=0 in master.
Same audit gate as AOMRidge-Blender. 117 source rows, 53 datasets.

Promotion blocked until completion run lands and rule in
build_benchmark_synthesis.assign_maturity is updated.

D-B-011 NO-GO for promotion (Codex round 3 APPROVE 2026-05-05).
Median +12.6 % vs aom_ridge_curated_best on audit20; q90 +57.1 %;
worst +102.7 %. Beats paper CNN by median −5.5 % (9/17 wins) and
ties paper CatBoost (10/20 wins). exhaustive_research only.

D-B-015 LOCKED 2026-05-07 (Codex round 6 APPROVE; supersedes
D-B-014 audit20 lock). FCK kernels integrated into AOM-PLS bank:
9 compact ops + 8 FCK ops = 17 total. FCK selected on 17/57
datasets (29.8 %) in full-57. Strict §3.3 gate FAILS: median
+8.7 % vs aom_ridge_curated_best (gate ≤ +5 %), q90 +35.8 %
(gate ≤ +25 %), worst +136.6 % (gate ≤ +75 %). Paper-CNN scoped
claim: median −5.5 %, 28/48 wins (58 %, below 75 % science gate).
exhaustive_research only; do NOT promote.
Evidence:
  bench/fck_pls/runs/aom_with_fck_fast12/results.csv (n=12)
  bench/fck_pls/runs/aom_with_fck_audit20/results.csv (n=20)
  bench/fck_pls/runs/aom_with_fck_full57/results.csv (n=57)

D-B-017b LOCKED via Codex round 8 APPROVE (2026-05-08). CV-tuned
AOM-Ridge with the 17-op `compact_with_fck` bank (9 compact + 8 FCK).
The fair comparison Codex round-7 demanded; supersedes D-B-017's
default-hyperparam audit. Strict +5 % gate FAILS at median +13.4 %
vs aom_ridge_curated_best (n=39); q90 +45.9 % (-10.7 pp better than
compact-only); worst +3173 % (-4278 pp better, but Quartz_spxy70
reference rmsep ≈ 3e-9 dominates the worst ratio so q90 is the
cleaner anchor); 4/39 wins (+1 over compact-only).
vs paper CNN: median -2.8 %, 26/48 wins (54.2 %) — beats paper CNN.
vs paper Ridge/PLS/CatBoost/TabPFN-raw: tied/slightly behind
(+5.9/+4.8/+3.9/+4.1 % median).
Stability trade-off (HYPOTHESIS, not proven by aggregate numbers):
the 8 added FCK blocks make Ridge more conservative ; sacrifices
small median for substantially more robust tails.
exhaustive_research only ; do NOT promote, recommend, prefer, or
describe as better than aom_ridge_curated_best.
Evidence:
  bench/fck_pls/runs/aom_with_fck_full57/results.csv
  (399 data rows; 114 AOMRidgePLSCV-* rows; 57 compact + 57 compact-with-fck)

D-B-016 LOCKED NO-GO via Codex round 7 APPROVE (2026-05-08 ~20:00 CEST).
Full-57 results: median +11.0 % vs aom_ridge_curated_best (FAILS strict
gate by 6.0 pp), q90 +32.2 %, worst +131.4 %, 3/39 wins. Paper-CNN
descriptive: median −3.2 %, 28/48 wins (58.3 %) — weaker than r21
V2L-Residual-AOMPLS's −9.8 %, 71 % wins. The "linear pendant" of r21
V2L-Residual gives a consistent but weaker NO-GO; FCK-static feature
extractor + Ridge head captures less of V2L-CNN's non-linear edge.
Closes the FCK × AOM matrix (preprocessing, in-bank, residual all NO-GO
vs strict AOM-Ridge gate, all paper-CNN-tied).
exhaustive_research only ; do NOT promote.
Evidence:
  bench/fck_pls/runs/aom_with_fck_audit20/results.csv (n=20, FCKResidual-AOMPLS)
  bench/fck_pls/runs/aom_with_fck_full57/results.csv (n=57, FCKResidual-AOMPLS)

D-A-009 k-tuning sweep variant (Codex round 12 LOCK 2026-05-08 17:10 CEST).
Same AOMLocalRidge class as `AOMRidge-Local-compact-knn50` (the D-A-003
k=50-only baseline) but with internal `k_grid: [10, 25, 50, 100, 200]`
sweep selecting best k per fit. Single-seed protocol (D-A-003 demonstrated
bit-identical RMSEPs across seeds 0/1/2 for this deterministic atom).
Diagnostic for the Chla+b_spxyG_species small-big-n boundary regression
(n_train=3734, R²=-13.45 at k=50). Cohort: same 4 big-n datasets as D-A-003.
A's manifest at bench/AOM_v0/Ridge/scenarios/da009_local_knn_sweep.json
reuses the `AOMRidge-Local-compact-knn50` canonical_name with the
kgrid-sweep YAML for registry-validation parity (workaround documented in
A's manifest description). This registry entry is the formal Codex-R12-§3
preset path for any future generic k-sweep runs.

Phase-11 ASL with atom-set guard active. Drops AOM-PLS-compact and
lazy-V2-AOM from the NNLS atom pool; keeps {multiK-3-5-7,
moe-preproc-soft}. Use only for n_train > 3000 datasets. Result
rows are tagged `extras.atom_guard=true` so the synthesis can keep
them out of the apples-to-apples comparison with the unguarded
ASL. Source: D-A-002 LOCKED in
`bench/AOM_v0/multiview/docs/PHASE11_PARTIAL_RUN.md` §3.1.

D-B-011 NO-GO. AOM-Ridge deltas worse than FCK-AOMPLS-static
(+13.8 % median, +98.5 % q90, +159.5 % worst, 1/15 wins) but
absolute median rmsep beats PLS-baseline by +15.92 %, the largest
cohort improvement among FCK pipelines. exhaustive_research only.
